steps.title: Translate the relationship into an equation
steps.content:
Let $n$ be the smaller integer. The larger integer is $7$ greater than twice the smaller, so it equals $2n+7$. Their product is $372$:
$$n(2n+7)=372$$

steps.title: Solve the quadratic
steps.content:
Expand and rearrange to standard form:
$$2n^2+7n-372=0$$
Apply the quadratic formula with $a=2$, $b=7$, $c=-372$:
$$n=\frac{-7\pm\sqrt{49+4(2)(372)}}{4}=\frac{-7\pm\sqrt{3025}}{4}=\frac{-7\pm 55}{4}$$
The positive root is $n=\frac{48}{4}=12$.

steps.title: Verify
steps.content:
If $n=12$, the larger integer is $2(12)+7=31$, and $12\times 31=372$, which matches.
The smaller integer is 12, so the answer is 12.
